notes:
Despite it having been 9 months since the prior network test the developers seem to have chosen to not enable EAC support. I have reached out to the developers on Steam forums, on Twitter as well as via their official support page both prior to and since the launch of the network test to ask about support and they have chosen to not respond to me within a timely manner.

Considering the state of many other Gundam games is also "borked" for the same exact reason, despite being different developers, I would argue that if they can't be bothered to enable it within 9 months for their second network test that they're simply refusing to do so with this lack of response and therefore it should be given up on. Sucks for those who wanted to play this game but either run only Linux or Steam Deck. 

I have also tried to run this with the standard Steam recommended version of Proton as well as forced Proton Experimental with similar success.
Can Install and run the game but stuck at black screen after EAC Logo.

This may not necessarily be an issue with EAC in and of itself--Given that there's no specific EAC error thrown either in a dialog (like Gundam Evolution used to) or in the console, the fact that the entire game refuses to start is a point of serious concern.

Further investigation is needed.
EAC pops up, loads, and the game boots to a black screen. Seems like they haven't enabled Linux support for EAC yet, even after the game's full release.

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6600 XT (navi23, LLVM 15.0.7, DRM 3.52, 6.4.11-arch2-1)
notes: Easy Anticheat loads without issue. When the game itself launches, it remains on a black screen.

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (gfx1100, LLVM 16.0.6, DRM 3.54, 6.5.5-zen1-1-zen)
notes: Can install the game & EAC but the game hangs on boot
